articles.title: Colossal 50-Foot Snake Vasuki indicus Unearthed in India Rivals Titanoboa
articles.summary: Fossils of Vasuki indicus, a 47-million-year-old snake up to 50 feet long, were found in Gujarat, India. This giant ambush predator rivals Titanoboa in size and sheds light on ancient snake evolution.

articles.title: From Lab Joke to 'Smart Underwear': Sensor Tracks Daily Farts to Establish a Normal Baseline
articles.summary: A quarter-sized sensor that clips into underwear measures hydrogen from farts. In a pilot study, people averaged 32 toots daily, with a range from 4 to 59. Researchers aim to define normal flatus levels.

articles.title: Bat-Inspired Drone Uses Echolocation to Fly Through Fog and Clutter
articles.summary: A $400 palm-sized drone navigates dark, smoky environments using ultrasound echolocation. Inspired by bumblebee bats, it could be used in search-and-rescue missions where cameras fail.
